have

constructor
  · -- Prove the forward direction: if biasedEnergyDiff net x i = 0, then biasedUpdateState net x i = x
    intro h
    by_contra h₁
    -- If biasedUpdateState net x i ≠ x, then we have a contradiction because biasedEnergyDiff net x i would be < 0
    have h₂ : biasedUpdateState net x i ≠ x := h₁
    have h₃ : biasedLocalField net x i ≠ 0 := by
      by_contra h₄
      -- If biasedLocalField net x i = 0, then biasedUpdateState net x i = x
      have h₅ : biasedLocalField net x i = 0 := by tauto
      have h₆ : biasedUpdateState net x i = x := by
        have h₇ : biasedLocalField net x i = 0 := h₅
        have h₈ : biasedUpdateState net x i = x := by
          -- If biasedLocalField net x i = 0, the update leaves x unchanged
          simp [biasedUpdateState, h₇]
          <;>
          (try cases x i <;> simp_all [if_pos, if_neg, biasedLocalField, localField, toRealVector, weightsMatrix,
            Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.dotProduct, Fin.sum_univ_succ, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ, Finset.sum_range_succ,
            Finset.sum_range_zero]) <;>
          (try contradiction) <;>
          (try ring_nf at * <;> norm_num at * <;> linarith)
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])
          <;>
          (try omega)
          <;>
          (try ring_nf at * <;> norm_num at * <;> linarith)
          <;>
          (try simp_all [Fin.ext_iff])
          <;>
          (try omega)
        exact h₈
      contradiction

-- If the i-th component is the same, and the rest are unchanged, then the entire state is the same
        have h₈ : biasedUpdateState net x i i = x i := h₆
        have h₉ : ∀ j : Fin n, (biasedUpdateState net x i j) = (x j) := by
          intro j
          by_cases h : j = i
          · -- Case: j = i
            simp_all [Function.update_same]
          · -- Case: j ≠ i
            have h₁₀ : (biasedUpdateState net x i j) = (x j) := by
              simp_all [Function.update_noteq]
            exact h₁₀
        ext j
        apply h₉
      exact h₇
    | inr h₄ =>
      -- Case: biasedLocalField net x i = 0
      have h₅ : biasedLocalField net x i = 0 := h₄
      have h₆ : biasedUpdateState net x i = x := by
        -- If the local field is zero, then the update does not change the state
        have h₇ : biasedLocalField net x i = 0 := h₄
        have h₈ : (biasedUpdateState net x i i) = x i := by
          -- Check the update rule when the local field is zero
          by_cases h₉ : (x i : ℝ) = 1
          · -- Case: x i = 1
            simp_all [biasedUpdateState, biasedLocalField, localField, toRealVector, Fin.sum_univ_succ, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.dotProduct, Fin.succAbove, Finset.sum_const, Finset.card_fin]
            <;>
            (try { contradiction }) <;>
            (try { linarith }) <;>
            (try {
              split_ifs at * <;>
              (try { contradiction }) <;>
              (try { linarith })
            })
            <;>
            (try {
              norm_num at * <;>
              linarith
            })
          · -- Case: x i ≠ 1
            by_cases h₁₀ : (x i : ℝ) = -1
            · -- Subcase: x i = -1
              simp_all [biasedUpdateState, biasedLocalField, localField, toRealVector, Fin.sum_univ_succ, Matrix.mulVec, Matrix.dotProduct, Fin.succAbove, Finset.sum_const, Finset.card_fin]
              <;>
              (try { contradiction }) <;>
              (try { linarith }) <;>
              (try {
                split_ifs at * <;>
                (try { contradiction }) <;>
                (try { linarith })
              })
              <;>
              (try {
                norm_num at * <;>
                linarith
              })
            · -- Subcase: x i ≠ -1
              exfalso

have h_main : biasedEnergyDiff net x i = 0 ↔ biasedUpdateState net x i = x := by
    -- Directly prove the equivalence by considering the cases where the state changes or not.
    by_cases h : biasedUpdateState net x i = x
    · -- Case: biasedUpdateState net x i = x
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if the energy difference is 0, then the state is fixed (already true by h)
        intro h₁
        exact h
      · -- Prove the backward direction: if the state is fixed, then the energy difference is 0
        intro h₁
        simp_all [biasedEnergyDiff]
        <;>
        (try ring_nf) <;>
        (try simp_all [biasedUpdateState, biasedLocalField, localField, toRealVector, Fin.sum_univ_succ, Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_succ]) <;>
        (try norm_num) <;>
        (try linarith)
    · -- Case: biasedUpdateState net x i ≠ x
      constructor
      · -- Prove the forward direction: if the energy difference is 0, then the state is fixed (contradicts h)
        intro h₁
        exfalso
